run_summary.note: battery process crashed on arm C (IncompleteRead) before per-arm persistence existed; metrics recomputed from this arm's committed log, meter splits from captured stdout
battery process crashed on arm C (IncompleteRead) before per-arm persistence existed; metrics recomputed from this arm's committed log, meter splits from captured stdout

P1.reason: degenerate guard: failure_rate(A)=0.0 < 0.10 — nothing to halve; stock already fits

unpredicted_findings.yield_inversion: Compacting the prompt REDUCED survivor yield ~37% on flash: stock 14.29 survivors/10k vs terse 8.93 and compact 8.99. The verbose stock instruction block is not burden for the small model, it is scaffolding: terse arms produced proportionally more refuted candidates (B 27/54, C 18/45 vs A 16/60) and more refuted-on-arrival skeleton failures (B 0.037 vs A 0.0167).
unpredicted_findings.truncation_hits_the_large_model: The only instruction-following failures at stock burden were on v4-pro (control): 3/5 calls truncated at max_tokens=4000 (avg completion 3535 tokens vs flash 2171). At this budget the compliance bottleneck is completion-length truncation on verbose models, not prompt comprehension on small ones. The small-model-optimisation premise inverted: flash is the tier that fits the harness as committed.
unpredicted_findings.transport_fault: http.client.IncompleteRead escaped HttpEndpoint.complete's retry net (HTTPException is outside its except tuple) and crashed the battery on arm C. Contained at the experiment layer (RobustEndpoint); the one-line call.py retry-tuple fix is a CANDIDATE citing this report, not applied. The crashed attempt's provider-side spend is unknowable and unlogged.

caveats: n=1 per arm, single seed, two problems (the committed smoke pair).
deepseek-v4-flash is a PROXY for the ~10B class (smallest model on the provided key; parameter count unpublished). Generalisation to true 10B checkpoints is unverified.

Arm C ran across two processes after the transport crash (16,203 + 13,830 tokens, matched 30k total); its meter_equals_log=false is the resume split, not a leak — replay_ok=true and the halves reconcile.
Arms A/B metrics were reconstructed from their committed logs after the process crash; meter splits from captured stdout (meter_equals_log unrecoverable, replay verified instead).
Arm D overshot to 33,615 tokens (documented check-before-spend overshoot; pro completions ~3.5k tokens).
Yield is count-based; survivor QUALITY was not judged (no judge in the loop, per prereg).
P3 is UNDECIDED as pre-registered (D made 5 < 8 calls); its failure_rate=0.6 is reported observationally only.
